title: Async Process Execution & Job Queue

transitions: QUEUED → STARTED (via startJob when runningJobs < maxJobs)
STARTED → FAILED (process failed or exception thrown)
QUEUED → ABORTED (canceler called on queued job)
STARTED → ABORTED (canceler called: sends SIGINT + stop(1))

activity_flow: Create React\Promise with resolver (captures resolve/reject into job) and canceler (sends SIGINT, stops process)
Attach .then() handlers: on-resolve → mark COMPLETED/FAILED → markJobDone(); on-reject → mark FAILED → markJobDone()
NO  → job stays QUEUED, return promise
Create Symfony\Process from command+cwd (fromShellCommandline or new Process)
[DECISION] Process construction threw? → call job.reject(e), return
React\Promise\all(promises) — aggregates all into one promise
[OPTIONAL] Init ProgressBar with total active jobs
IF status === STARTED AND process.isRunning() === false:
call job['resolve'](process) → triggers .then() → marks COMPLETED/FAILED → markJobDone() [runningJobs--]
IF runningJobs < maxJobs AND status === QUEUED: startJob(job.id)
IF index provided: return jobs[index].status < COMPLETED ? 1 : 0
ELSE: count jobs with status < COMPLETED; unset completed jobs from $jobs; return count
LOOP: if countActiveJobs(index) === 0 → return; else usleep(1000)
FOR EACH promise in currentPromises: promise.cancel()
Canceler: if QUEUED → ABORTED; if STARTED → ABORTED + SIGINT + stop(1) + throw RuntimeException
key_methods: ProcessExecutor::enableAsync(): void — sets allowAsync=true; must be called before executeAsync
ProcessExecutor::executeAsync(command, cwd?): PromiseInterface<Process> — queues job, starts if capacity allows
ProcessExecutor::startJob(int $id): void — transitions QUEUED→STARTED, creates & starts Symfony\Process
ProcessExecutor::countActiveJobs(?int $index): int — tick: resolves finished jobs, starts queued jobs, returns active count
ProcessExecutor::wait(?int $index): void — busy-waits via usleep(1000) until countActiveJobs==0
ProcessExecutor::resetMaxJobs(): void — reads COMPOSER_MAX_PARALLEL_PROCESSES env (1-50), default 10
Loop::wait(PromiseInterface[], ?ProgressBar): void — master event loop draining all active jobs
Loop::abortJobs(): void — cancels all tracked promises
concurrency_rules: maxJobs default=10, configurable via COMPOSER_MAX_PARALLEL_PROCESSES env (clamped 1-50)
runningJobs counter incremented in startJob, decremented in markJobDone
New jobs are auto-started in executeAsync IF capacity exists at enqueue time
Capacity re-checked on every countActiveJobs() tick for queued jobs
Promise canceler sends SIGINT + stop(1) to started processes
dependencies: Loop depends_on ProcessExecutor (delegates tick via countActiveJobs)
Loop depends_on HttpDownloader (parallel HTTP + process jobs in same loop)
important_notes: executeAsync() throws LogicException if enableAsync() was never called — caller MUST use the ProcessExecutor bound to a Loop instance. The event loop has NO sleep in Loop::wait (pure busy-wait), but ProcessExecutor::wait uses usleep(1000). countActiveJobs() is the single 'tick' method that both resolves finished processes AND starts queued ones — it must be called repeatedly. Completed/failed jobs are GC'd (unset) from $jobs[] inside countActiveJobs() only when no specific index is queried.
